message:
fix(packs/database/redis): close compound-command bypass via negative-lookahead forbid set

The redis safe patterns (GET/MGET/SCAN/INFO/KEYS/DBSIZE/CONFIG GET)
previously matched whenever those verbs appeared anywhere in the
command, even if a destructive Redis command was also present. A
compound invocation like:

  redis-cli FLUSHALL && redis-cli GET mykey
  redis-cli -e 'DEBUG SLEEP 0' GET mykey
  bash -c 'redis-cli SHUTDOWN; redis-cli KEYS "*"'

would match the safe `GET`/`KEYS` pattern and bypass the
destructive check — despite obviously being destructive.

Fix: prepend a shared negative-lookahead forbid set to every safe
pattern:

  const FORBID: &str = r"(?!.*\b(?:FLUSHALL|FLUSHDB|DEBUG|SHUTDOWN|
                         CONFIG\s+(?:SET|REWRITE))\b)";

Each safe pattern now becomes:
  safe_pattern!("redis-get", &format!(r"(?i){FORBID}\b(?:GET|MGET)\b"))

The lookahead inspects the entire command string at match time and
refuses to declare it safe if any destructive Redis keyword is
present, regardless of whether the safe verb appears before, after,
or alongside the destructive one. Kept FORBID as a `const &str`
pulled into every pattern via format!() so the forbid set has one
source of truth and additions to the destructive list only need to
touch one line.

The destructive patterns below are unchanged — they still match the
destructive half of compound commands; this commit only closes the
hole where the safe half was shadowing them.
